prerequisites: A SafetyCulture account on a Premium or Enterprise plan, which is required to generate API tokens.
A SafetyCulture API token (either a user API token or a service user API token) generated from the web app.

Knowledge that token access is scoped: requests can only return templates, inspections, assets, and other items the token's subject can access in SafetyCulture, so generate the token from an account or service user with access to the data you want to extract.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the SafetyCulture API using a single API token supplied in the api_key field. The token is sent on each request in the Authorization: Bearer <token> header against the base URL https://api.safetyculture.io. You can use either a personal user API token or a service user API token; service user tokens are recommended for long-term integrations because they are not tied to an individual user and do not count toward seat billing.

setupSteps.title: Generate a user API token
setupSteps.body: In the SafetyCulture web app, click your username in the lower-left corner and select My profile. Click Settings in the upper-right, then select API tokens. Click Generate API token, enter a token name and your account password, click Generate, and copy the token immediately and store it securely. User API tokens inherit your access and expire after 30 days of inactivity. This requires a full seat on a Premium or Enterprise plan.

setupSteps.title: (Alternative) Generate a service user API token
setupSteps.body: For long-term or shared integrations where access should remain stable regardless of user changes, create a service user API token instead. This requires the Platform management: Organization permission, a full seat, and a Premium or Enterprise plan. Service user tokens are admin-assigned, do not count toward seats, and have a configurable inactivity window (31, 45, 60, or 180 days). An organization can have up to 20 active service user tokens.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated token in the connector's required api_key field. The connector sends it as Authorization: Bearer <token> when calling the SafetyCulture API at https://api.safetyculture.io. No other configuration fields are required.
